fact_nuggets.text: Refrigerate (Japanese: Freeze Skin) is an Ability introduced in Generation VI. From Generation VIII onward, it is the signature Ability of Amaura and Aurorus.
Signature Ability of Amaura And Aurorus

Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description S MD Normal-type moves that the Pokemon uses become Ice type and deal more damage! MD RT DX Normal-type moves used by the Pokemon become Ice type and deal more damage.

Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generation VI 1.1.2 Generation VII 1.1.3 Generation VIII 1.1.4 Affected moves 1.2 Outside of battle Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generation VI [ edit | edit source ] Refrigerate causes all Normal-type moves used by the Pokemon to become Ice-type and receive a 30% power boost. Electrify is applied before Refrigerate, so if the Pokemon is affected by Electrify the move becomes Electric-type and does not have its power boosted by Refrigerate. The type-changing effect is applied before Ion Deluge , so if both effects are applying, an originally Normal-type move will end up as a Ice-type move (as it is no longer Normal-type when Ion Deluge would be applied). Generation VII [ edit | edit source ] Affected moves now receive a 20% power boost. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] This Ability effect may be in need of research. Reason: What happens if Gigantamax Lapras with Refrigerate tries to use Max Strike ? You can discuss this on the talk page. If Max Strike is selected with Refrigerate, it'll turn into Max Hailstorm when used. Affected moves [ edit | edit source ] The table below lists the moves affected by Refrigerate, as well as the Pokemon that can have Refrigerate and learn those moves. Move Type Cat. Power and Boost Learned by Body Slam Normal Physical 85 102 Breakneck Blitz Normal Physical Breakneck Blitz Normal Special Double-Edge Normal Physical 120 144 Echoed Voice Normal Special 40 48 Explosion Normal Physical 250 300 Facade Normal Physical 70 84 Frustration Normal Physical Giga Impact Normal Physical 150 180 Headbutt Normal Physical 70 84 Hyper Beam Normal Special 150 180 Hyper Voice Normal Special 90 108 Return Normal Physical Round Normal Special 60 72 Secret Power Normal Physical 70 84 Self-Destruct Normal Physical 200 240 Snore Normal Special 50 60 Take Down Normal Physical 90 108 Weather Ball Normal Special 50 60 Although the following moves are affected by the Ability, they currently cannot be learned by any Pokemon with Refrigerate. They may, however, be called via Metronome , Mirror Move , Copycat , or Mimic , or be used by a Pokemon that gained Refrigerate by an Ability like Trace or Receiver or a move such as Skill Swap or Role Play . Contents 1 In battle 1.1 Generation VI 1.2 Generation VII 1.3 Generation VIII 1.4 Affected moves In battle [ edit | edit source ] Generation VI [ edit | edit source ] Refrigerate causes all Normal-type moves used by the Pokemon to become Ice-type and receive a 30% power boost. Electrify is applied before Refrigerate, so if the Pokemon is affected by Electrify the move becomes Electric-type and does not have its power boosted by Refrigerate. The type-changing effect is applied before Ion Deluge , so if both effects are applying, an originally Normal-type move will end up as a Ice-type move (as it is no longer Normal-type when Ion Deluge would be applied). Generation VII [ edit | edit source ] Affected moves now receive a 20% power boost. Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] This Ability effect may be in need of research. Reason: What happens if Gigantamax Lapras with Refrigerate tries to use Max Strike ? You can discuss this on the talk page. If Max Strike is selected with Refrigerate, it'll turn into Max Hailstorm when used.
